message.content:
Search for the "back button" pattern used in the Easy Salon frontend project. I need to find how the "voltar" (back) button is implemented in other pages.

Search in: C:\Users\charl\OneDrive\Área de Trabalho\WA\Easy\easy-salon-monorepo\frontend\src

1. Use Grep to find files containing "voltar" or "ArrowLeft" or "ChevronLeft" combined with navigation/back behavior (case insensitive)
2. Read the first 2-3 files that show the back button pattern most clearly
3. Also check PageHeader component: C:\Users\charl\OneDrive\Área de Trabalho\WA\Easy\easy-salon-monorepo\frontend\src\components\layout\PageHeader.tsx

Return the exact JSX/TSX code for the back button pattern used, including imports and the component structure around it.

content.text: Now let me read the PageHeader component and examine a few of the top files that contain the back button pattern:
Now let me read a couple more files to see other implementations of the back button pattern:
Let me search for more specific implementations to see if there are variations:
Let me check the ProfilePage to see how the back button is used there:

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the back button patterns. Let me create a summary of my findings: ## Back Button Pattern Summary Based on my search of the Easy Salon frontend project, I found **two main implementations** of the back button pattern: ### Pattern 1: Direct Navigation Button with Icon (Most Common) This is used in pages like **WorkingHoursPage.tsx** and **ServiceEditPage.tsx**: ```tsx import { ArrowLeft } from "lucide-react"; import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button"; import { useNavigate } from "react-router-dom"; export default function SomePage() { const navigate = useNavigate(); return (

Page Title

Subtitle text

); } ``` **Key characteristics:** - Uses `ArrowLeft` icon from lucide-react - Uses secondary Button variant with icon-only size - Icon dimensions: `h-5 w-5` - Navigates to a specific route (e.g., `/salon/settings`) - Wrapped with title and subtitle in a flex container ### Pattern 2: Navigation Item with Back Action This is used in **ProfilePage.tsx**: ```tsx import { ArrowLeft } from "lucide-react"; import { useNavigate } from "react-router-dom"; export default function ProfilePage() { const navigate = useNavigate(); const profileNavItems = [ { title: "Voltar", href: "#", icon: ArrowLeft, onClick: () => navigate(-1) } ]; return ( {/* Page content */} ); } ``` **Key characteristics:** - Uses ArrowLeft icon with title "Voltar" (Back in Portuguese) - Uses browser history navigation `navigate(-1)` instead of hardcoded routes - Integrated into the side navigation as a nav item object - Useful for dynamic navigation back to previous pages ### PageHeader Component The **PageHeader.tsx** component (`C:\Users\charl\OneDrive\Área de Trabalho\WA\Easy\easy-salon-monorepo\frontend\src\components\layout\PageHeader.tsx`) doesn't have a built-in back button, but it's commonly used with the direct navigation button pattern above. ### Recommended Implementation For consistency with the existing codebase, use **Pattern 1** (direct navigation button) for most pages: ```tsx

Your Page Title

Subtitle

``` Files where this pattern is used: - C:\Users\charl\OneDrive\Área de Trabalho\WA\Easy\easy-salon-monorepo\frontend\src\pages\salon\settings\WorkingHoursPage.tsx (line 293-301) - C:\Users\charl\OneDrive\Área de Trabalho\WA\Easy\easy-salon-monorepo\frontend\src\pages\salon\ServiceEditPage.tsx - C:\Users\charl\OneDrive\Área de Trabalho\WA\Easy\easy-salon-monorepo\frontend\src\pages\salon\UserServicesPage.tsx
